hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: Breaking the Ice -- Small Talk at a Global Event
lesson.menu_desc: The dinner before the presentation: introducing yourself, saying what you are working on right now, and keeping the conversation alive -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- Breaking the Ice at a Global Event
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Aula 4 -- Breaking the Ice at a Global Event

listenings.text: Emmanuele, Marc Dubois. Good news. The dates for August are confirmed, and there is one thing before the presentation. On the first evening, we have a dinner. It is informal. No slides, no numbers. And I know this is the part you are afraid of. So here is my advice. Nobody at that table is currently thinking about your grammar. They are thinking about the food, and about the person next to them. Ask people what they are working on. That is the whole secret. And Emmanuele, I am looking forward to finally meeting you in person. Talk soon.

Good evening, everyone, and welcome to the immunology congress. My name is Claire Bennett, and I am opening this evening on behalf of the global team. Tonight is not a working session. Tonight is for the people. Around you there are doctors, executives, and colleagues from twenty-two countries. Some of them are launching a product this month. Some of them are preparing a new study. All of them are here because they want to talk to somebody new. So please, do not stay with your own team. Turn to the person next to you and introduce yourself. That is why we are all here. Enjoy the evening.

extra_audio.text: Hello. I do not think we have met. I am Emmanuele Orrico, from Sanofi Brazil. It is great to finally meet you in person. I am currently working on the immunology portfolio. And you? What are you working on at the moment? That is very interesting. I am looking forward to hearing more about it tomorrow.

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a senior leader who makes the big decisions in a company
to say who you are, or to present one person to another
easy, friendly conversation before the real business starts
the new medicines a company is developing, before they reach the market
a person who works with you, in your company or in another one
a big professional event where doctors and companies meet
a person or a group with an interest in your project
to meet new people at an event and build professional contacts

reading.rtitle: The Dinner Is Part of the Congress
paras: Every year, thousands of people go to a medical congress. They listen to the presentations, they take notes, and they go home. But the executives who work in global companies know a secret: the real congress does not happen in the presentation room. It happens at the dinner, in the coffee line, and in the ten minutes before a session starts.

This is the moment for small talk. Small talk is not empty conversation. It is a door: you open it with an easy question, and the business walks through it. "What brings you to this congress?" is a better first question than any number. And the answer is almost always the same shape: "I am currently working on..." People love to talk about the project they are living with right now.

So do not stay with your own team, and do not wait for the perfect sentence. Introduce yourself, ask what the other person is working on, and listen. You are not selling anything tonight. You are networking: you are meeting colleagues, executives and stakeholders, and you are telling them, in one simple sentence, what you do. Tomorrow, in the meeting room, they already know your name.

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: A congress is only useful if you attend every presentation and take good notes.
The most valuable part of a congress happens outside the presentation room -- in the small talk, where you introduce yourself and ask people what they are working on.
You should stay with your own team at a congress, because it is safer and more comfortable.

items: The text says the real congress happens inside the presentation room.
The opposite: it happens at the dinner, in the coffee line, and in the ten minutes before a session starts.
Small talk is described as a door that opens the way to business.
The second paragraph says small talk is not empty conversation -- it is a door, and the business walks through it.
"What brings you to this congress?" is a good way to start.
The text says it is a better first question than any number.
The text says you should sell your product at the dinner.
It says the opposite: "You are not selling anything tonight. You are networking."

gapfill.text: Notice the shape of the answer everybody gives at a congress: I am working on... / We are launching... / They are preparing... This is the structure of NOW. It is the sentence you will say fifty times in August.

lf.text: A and C are always true -- they are your job, and they do not change: present simple. B and D are true THIS MONTH, and next month they change: am / is / are + verb-ING. Both are correct English. They simply answer different questions.

bank.label: Useful language for breaking the ice
items: Hello. I do not think we have met. I am Emmanuele, from Sanofi Brazil.
It is great to finally meet you in person.
What brings you to this congress?
I am currently working on the immunology portfolio.
What are you working on at the moment?
I am looking forward to working with your team.

items.situation: You are alone at the dinner table. The executive next to you turns to you. Open the conversation.
tips: Hello. I do not think we have met. I am Emmanuele, from Sanofi Brazil.
Good evening. May I introduce myself? I am Emmanuele Orrico.

items.situation: "So, what brings you to this congress?"
tips: I am here for the immunology sessions. I work in the Brazilian team.
I am currently working on the immunology portfolio, so this congress is important for me.

items.situation: "And what are you working on at the moment?"
tips: Right now, I am preparing the presentation for the August meeting.
I am currently working on the asthma launch with my field team.

items.situation: You want to know what the other person does. Ask the question.
tips: And you? What are you working on at the moment?
What is your team preparing this year?

items.situation: The colleague says: "We are launching a new product in Europe this month." React, and keep the conversation alive.
tips: That is very interesting. Is the launch going well?
That is interesting. We are preparing a launch in Brazil too.

items.situation: The conversation is ending and you want to see this person again tomorrow. Close it, and use the expression from today.
tips: I am looking forward to working with your team.
It was great to meet you. I am looking forward to your session tomorrow.
